title: Complementary health associate professionals
description: Conduct research in the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; research ways to make processed foods safe, palatable, and healthful; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Conduct research in breeding, physiology, production, yield, and management of crops and agricultural plants or trees, shrubs, and nursery stock, their growth in soils, and control of pests; or study the chemical, physical, biological, and mineralogical composition of soils as they relate to plant or crop growth. May classify and map soils and investigate effects of alternative practices on soil and crop productivity. (Based on 39 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Assess clients' soft tissue condition, joint quality and function, muscle strength, and range of motion, and document patient information, such as notes on their progress. Develop and propose client treatment plans that specify which types of massage are to be used and confer with clients about their medical histories and problems with stress or pain to determine how massage will be most helpful. Collaborate with or assist doctors, psychologists, or rehabilitation therapists in working with patients with cognitive, intellectual, or developmental disabilities to treat, rehabilitate, and return patients to the community. Communicate and collaborate with other healthcare professionals involved with the care of a patient and refer clients to other types of therapists when necessary.

Fit patients for orthopedic braces, prostheses, or supportive devices, such as crutches. Instruct, motivate, safeguard, and assist patients as they practice exercises or functional activities. Train patients in the use of orthopedic braces, prostheses, or supportive devices and provide clients with guidance and information about techniques for postural improvement and stretching, strengthening, relaxation, and rehabilitative exercises.

Massage and knead muscles and soft tissues of the body to provide treatment for medical conditions, injuries, or wellness maintenance. Apply finger and hand pressure to specific points of the body and administer active or passive manual therapeutic exercises, therapeutic massage, aquatic physical therapy, or heat, light, sound, or electrical modality treatments, such as ultrasound. Perform other adjunctive therapies or treatment techniques in addition to massage, and teach patients to use home health care equipment.

Maintain treatment records and communicate with or instruct caregivers or family members on patient therapeutic activities or treatment plans. Perform clerical duties, such as taking inventory, ordering supplies, answering telephone, taking messages, or filling out forms, and ensure cleanliness of work area by restocking supplies or sanitizing equipment after treatment.

Transport patients to and from treatment areas, lifting and transferring them according to positioning requirements. Monitor operation of equipment and record use of equipment and administration of treatment. Secure patients into or onto therapy equipment and clean work area and check and store equipment after treatment.

Observe patients during treatments to compile and evaluate data on their responses and progress and provide results to physical therapist in person or through progress notes. Monitor patients' physical and emotional well-being and report unusual behavior or physical ailments to medical staff. Assess clients to ensure appropriate care and treatment is provided throughout the process.

Attend or conduct continuing education courses, seminars, or in-service activities to maintain knowledge of community services and resources available to patients and keep abreast of developments in the field. Maintain proper professional standards and practice guidelines. Participate in research projects, conferences, or technical meetings for professional development.

Assist patients to dress, undress, or put on and remove supportive devices, such as braces, splints, or slings. Aid patients in performing tasks, such as bathing or keeping beds, clothing, or living areas clean, and encourage patients to develop work skills and to participate in social, recreational, or other therapeutic activities.

Conduct nutritional assessments of individuals, including obtaining and evaluating individuals' dietary histories, to plan nutritional programs. Develop or teach strategies to promote client wellness and independence, and educate patients on ophthalmic medical procedures, conditions of the eye, and appropriate use of medications.

Perform general administrative tasks, such as scheduling appointments or ordering supplies or equipment, and assist in training technicians, medical students, residents, or other staff members. Maintain files and records of procedures or treatments to ensure compliance with best practices and regulations.
Conduct research into the genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals and analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein.
Educate patients and their families about treatment plans, physiological treatments, nutrition, and disease management techniques.
Monitor patients' progress, comfort, safety, dietary intake, and metabolic responses to treatment, consulting with physicians as necessary about adverse reactions or changes in health.
Educate patients on healthy eating habits, therapeutic exercise, and nutritional regimens, and assist in meal planning based on nutritional needs and established guidelines.
Analyze menus or recipes, standardize recipes, or test new food products for proper nutritional content and safety.
Monitor maternal and fetal well-being during pregnancy and labor, providing necessary medical care and counseling about pregnancy processes and nutritional needs.

Collaborate in research studies. Incorporate research findings into practice as appropriate. Participate in clinical research projects. Present or publish scientific papers. Write applications for research grants. Conduct literature reviews. Write articles for publication in professional journals. Write up or orally communicate research findings to the scientific community, producers, and the public. Develop, or make recommendations on, management systems and plans for wildlife populations and habitat. Prepare or review reports, manuscripts, or meeting presentations. Share research findings by writing scientific articles or by making presentations at scientific conferences. Manage laboratory teams or monitor the quality of a team's work.

Provide information about community health and social resources. Recommend the use of vitamin and mineral supplements to enhance the health of patients and children. Treat patients' symptoms with alternative health care methods such as herbs or hydrotherapy. Provide patients with contraceptive and family planning information. Perform annual gynecologic exams, including pap smears and breast exams. Evaluate patients' laboratory and medical records, requesting assistance from other practitioners when necessary. Provide instructions to patients or family members concerning diagnoses or treatment plans. Refer patients to ophthalmic surgeons or other physicians. Develop or use special test and communication techniques to facilitate diagnosis and treatment of children or patients with disabilities. Conduct in-service training sessions to disseminate information regarding equipment or instruments.

Develop, implement, or evaluate individualized plans for midwifery care. Respond to breech birth presentations by applying methods such as exercises or external version. Identify, monitor, or treat pregnancy-related problems such as hypertension, gestational diabetes, pre-term labor, or retarded fetal growth. Monitor patient intra-operative status, including patient position, vital signs, or volume and color of blood. Prepare and apply sterile wound dressings. Evaluate the effects of drugs, gases, pesticides, parasites, and microorganisms at various levels. Follow strict safety procedures when handling toxic materials to avoid contamination. Inspect food processing areas to ensure compliance with government regulations and standards for sanitation, safety, quality, and waste management.

Analyze determinants responsible for specific inherited traits, and devise methods for altering traits or producing new traits. Study the structure and composition of food or the changes foods undergo in storage and processing. Oversee public health programs, including statistical analysis, health care planning, surveillance systems, and public health improvement. Analyze food processing and storage operations and assist in the development of quality assurance programs for such operations. Conduct research to develop methodologies, instrumentation, and procedures for medical application, analyzing data and presenting findings. Develop new food items for production, based on consumer feedback. Test new products for flavor, texture, color, nutritional content, and adherence to government and industry standards. Research and control animal selection and breeding practices to increase production efficiency and improve animal quality. Study management practices, processing methods, feed, or environmental conditions on quality and quantity of animal products, such as eggs and milk.

Conduct research in genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals. Use chemistry, microbiology, engineering, and other sciences to study the principles underlying the processing and deterioration of foods; analyze food content to determine levels of vitamins, fat, sugar, and protein; discover new food sources; and apply food science knowledge to determine best ways to process, package, preserve, store, and distribute food. Study effects of management practices, processing methods, feed, or environmental conditions on quality and quantity of animal products, such as eggs and milk. Develop new or improved ways of preserving, processing, packaging, storing, and delivering foods, using knowledge of chemistry, microbiology, and other sciences.

Identify and analyze public health issues related to foodborne parasitic diseases and their impact on public policies, scientific studies, or surveys. Consult with and advise physicians, educators, researchers, government health officials, and others regarding medical applications of sciences, such as physics, biology, and chemistry. Develop pest management and control measures, and conduct risk assessments related to pest exclusion, using scientific methods. Study reactions of plants, animals, and marine species to parasites. Investigate cause, progress, life cycle, or mode of transmission of diseases or parasites. Study methods to improve aspects of foods, such as chemical composition, flavor, color, texture, nutritional value, and convenience. Collaborate with ophthalmologists, optometrists, or other specialists in the diagnosis, treatment, or management of conditions such as glaucoma, cataracts, and retinal diseases.

Develop and maintain liaisons and effective working relations with groups and individuals, agencies, and the public to encourage cooperative management strategies or to develop information and interpret findings.

Perform support duties, such as preparing materials, keeping records, maintaining supplies, and scheduling activities. Collect and compile data to document clients' performance or assess program quality. Document clients' progress toward meeting established treatment objectives.

Test or maintain equipment to ensure correct performance. Use equipment such as atomic absorption spectrometers, electron microscopes, flow cytometers, or chromatography systems. Collect specimens from patients, using standard medical procedures. Prepare charts, graphs, or other visual displays to communicate clients' performance information. Assist physicians or registered nurses in the conduct of endoscopic procedures. Assist members of the surgical team with gowning or gloving. Gather, arrange, or assemble instruments or supplies. Prepare suites or rooms according to endoscopic procedure requirements. Maintain or repair endoscopic equipment. Monitor and maintain aseptic technique throughout procedures. Clean, disinfect, or calibrate scopes or other endoscopic instruments.

Write grant proposals to obtain funding for biological research. Write grant applications to obtain funding. Prepare technical and research reports, such as environmental impact reports, and communicate the results to individuals in industry, government, or the general public.

Conduct research in genetics, nutrition, reproduction, growth, and development of domestic farm animals and crops; study the principles underlying food processing and deterioration; develop methods for food safety, palatability, and nutritional value; analyze soil composition and its effects on plant growth; research and advise on agricultural practices and environmental impacts of land and water use.

Teach or supervise students and perform research at universities and colleges; instruct medical students or healthcare workers in methods for diagnosis and management of genetic disorders and other biological processes; communicate research results through presentations and publications.

Analyze characteristics of animals and plants to identify and classify them; study basic principles of life such as anatomy and physiology, species interactions, and growth; investigate relationships between organisms and disease, including the effects of antibiotics and other substances.

Research the environmental effects of agricultural practices and develop improved methods for crop yield, pest management, and sustainable farming; investigate soil characteristics and develop techniques for soil conservation and management; classify and analyze data related to soil productivity.

Collect and analyze biological data about relationships among organisms and their environment; study nutritional requirements of animals and analyze the nutritive values of animal feed materials; conduct chemical analyses related to agricultural products and soil health.

Participate in or supervise biological research programs, providing technical support and guidance to other scientists and technicians; collaborate with engineers on agricultural projects; represent the employer at conferences and public events.

Monitor and test food and water for harmful microorganisms, pollution, and analyze impacts of environmental conditions; develop guidelines for food safety and environmental health practices; educate the public on health and safety standards.

Develop or execute tests to detect diseases and investigate genetic factors affecting agricultural products; supervise laboratory activities and ensure safety protocols are maintained; write technical reports based on research findings.

Analyze and improve laboratory and field methods for research; collaborate on the development of new products and procedures for agricultural practices; keep up with technological advances in bioproduct development and genetic research.

Conduct experiments to investigate plant growth mechanisms in relation to environmental changes; develop new agricultural techniques or products to enhance sustainability; evaluate new technologies and methodologies in agricultural research.

emerging_tasks.task: Attend high-risk and caesarian section infant deliveries to provide neonatal respiratory care as needed.
Perform general administrative tasks, such as answering telephones, scheduling appointments, or ordering supplies or equipment.
Transport patients to and from the operating room.
Test patients for sexually transmitted infections.
Perform tests on water, food, and the environment to detect harmful microorganisms or to obtain information about sources of pollution, contamination, or infection and interpret the results.
Teach epidemiology to students in public health programs.
Test processing equipment to ensure products are produced according to specifications.
Use advanced technologies, such as GIS, remote sensing, and drone technology, for wildlife tracking, habitat mapping, and population studies.
